completeness.bounded_by: OSRS Wiki Ironman_money_making_guide: 39 enumerable money-making table rows (Recommended 20 / Low requirements 13 / High requirements 6) form the bounded universe; prose sections (Early, Mid-Late, Selling to shops, Treasure Trails) add further non-enumerable methods captured as additional records.
known_missing: BOUNDED UNIVERSE STATUS: all 39 enumerable table rows (Recommended 20 / Low 13 / High 6) are captured -> ZERO bounded-universe gap. Items below are clarifications/informational, not dropped methods.

Clue casket GE-value column rendered values (beginner 3,220.01 / easy 33,356.20 / medium 137,453.09 / hard 133,042.09 / elite 288,711.51 / master 726,079.46) captured in the Treasure Trails record's outputs as informational/main-only; not iron-realizable (GE).

Clue scroll SOURCE drop-rate table (Common Sources of Clue Scrolls: Puro-Puro implings, H.A.M. Members, Scurrius, Jelly, Chambers of Xeric, Dorgesh-Kaan/Aldarin/Rogues' Castle chests, Corrupted Gauntlet, crabs, guards, Sarachnis, Barracuda salvage) is a clue-acquisition reference, not a money method; rates summarized in Treasure Trails record account_note rather than as separate records.
Casket HA-value cells use wiki {{Average drop value}} templates; rendered HA figures captured from WebFetch snapshot (price-volatile).

domain_stats.note_table_vs_record_count: Several table rows are merged with their Early/Mid-Late prose mentions into a single record (e.g. Wintertodt appears in prose + Recommended table; eclipse red, steel platebodies, agility pyramid, selling cats appear in both Early prose and Low table). Records also include prose-only methods with no table row. Hence record_count != raw table-row count.

_provenance.fix_note: P1 gate fix (2026-06-17): added audience='ironman', pricing_basis (derived from realization_channel: coins/high_alch/shop/mixed identity), and requires_ge=false to all 49 records; corrected one out-of-enum value_basis 'mixed' -> 'store' on the steel platebodies record (fixed per-item shop/high-alch prices). No new fetches; no records added/removed.

records.method: Stronghold of Security (loot chests)

items: food to survive high-level monster attacks

records.risk: high-level monsters in dungeon; run past them
records.account_note: Earliest starting cash available to ALL account types (main/iron/uim, members & f2p). Direct coins, iron-realizable.
records.value_note: Fixed one-time 10,000 coins. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Pickpocketing men and women in Lumbridge (early Thieving)

outputs.gold: small amounts (direct coins)

records.account_note: Trainable immediately; quickest way to accrue small gold; levels Thieving toward blackjacking / stealing valuables. Direct coins, iron-realizable.
records.value_note: Qualitative 'small amounts'; no hourly given. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Wintertodt (early)

outputs.gold: cash

records.account_note: Early game boss generating skilling supplies and cash without food/potions. Mixed (gold + XP + resources); iron-realizable. See Recommended-table Wintertodt row for hourly figures.
records.value_note: Qualitative in prose; hourly profit quantified in Recommended table. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Selling cats for death runes (West Ardougne)

quests: Easy Ardougne Diary (for 200 death runes; 100 before)

outputs.gold: ~21,600 coins worth of death runes every few hours if sold to Aubury (Varrock)
outputs.value_if_kept: 36,000 coins saved if death runes used rather than bought

records.account_note: Death runes either sold to Aubury (coins) or kept as runes (value saved). 'mixed' = coins OR resource value. Iron-realizable (shop sale to Aubury / self-use). Also appears in Low-requirements table at 13,300 gp/hr.
records.source_section: Early Money Making (prose) + Low requirements table
records.value_note: 13,300 gp/hr equiv assumes runes valued at 200 coins each (saved cost). gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Picking up ground spawns and selling to specialty/general stores (general)

outputs.gold: near high-alch value per item from shops

records.account_note: Ground spawns picked up via world-hopping, sold to specialty shops or general stores (e.g. Pollnivneach general store buys near high-alch value). Shop channel, iron-realizable. Sub-spawns below.
records.value_note: Near high-alch value at shops; qualitative. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Selling Eclipse red (Varlamore) at Moonrise Wines

outputs.gold: 700 coins per bottle of eclipse red wine; ~400 wines/hr

records.account_note: Eclipse red ground-spawn in Guildmaster Apatura's quarters (Hunter Guild; no Hunter req for spawn or bank chest). Sold to Moonrise Wines at fixed 700gp regardless of stock. Shop channel, iron-realizable. Also Low-requirements table 'Gathering eclipse red' at 280,000 gp/hr.
records.source_section: Early Money Making (prose) + Low requirements table
records.value_note: 700 coins/bottle fixed shop price; ~400/hr assumed. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Collecting steel platebodies (Wilderness) and selling to Horvik / general store / high-alch

outputs.gold: general store 800; Horvik's Armour Shop 1,200; high-alch 1,200 per steel platebody
outputs.note: Lava Maze level 40-45 Wilderness also has staff of earth spawn

records.risk: Wilderness (lvl 40-45 Lava Maze); player killers; not recommended for HCIM; UIM only viable very early
records.account_note: Steel platebody spawn in Lava Maze. Realized via general store/Horvik (shop) OR high-alch -> mixed channels. Iron-realizable. Safer alt: steel platelegs in Eastern Ruins (lvl 25-28 Wild): general store 400 / Louie's 650 / high-alch 600. Lower-risk alts: bronze chainbodies (Dwarven Mines) or iron swords (Briget's) make <20,000 gp/hr. UIM badge: Maybe. Also Low-requirements table 'Collecting steel platebodies' 128,000 gp/hr.
records.source_section: Early Money Making (prose) + Low requirements table
records.value_note: Per-item shop/alch prices fixed; 128,000 gp/hr aggregate. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Picking up ruby rings (Varrock west bank) and high-alching

items: fire/nature runes for High Level Alchemy

outputs.gold: from high-alching ruby rings

records.account_note: Ruby ring spawn after Crack the Clue III; respawn ~240s so profit depends heavily on competition; hourly includes time to high-alch the rings. Also sellable to Grum (Varrock west bank) per prose. high_alch channel, iron-realizable. Recommended table top entry.
records.source_section: Recommended Methods table (+ Early prose 'Ruby rings to Grum')
records.value_note: 400,000 gp/hr competition-dependent; includes alch time. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Agility Pyramid

outputs.gold: 10,000 coins per successful lap (turn pyramid tops to Simon Templeton); up to ~200,000 coins/hr

records.account_note: Unlocks at 30 Agility but 50-70+ recommended for success rate. Direct coins from Simon Templeton, iron-realizable. UIM badge: Yes (no banking needed, reliable early method). Listed 100,000+ gp/hr (up to ~200k). Both Early prose and Low-requirements table.
records.source_section: Early Money Making (prose) + Low requirements table
records.value_note: 10,000 coins/lap fixed; 100,000-200,000 gp/hr range. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Selling items to Martin Thwait (Rogues' Den) at High Alchemy price

outputs.gold: High Alchemy price per item via Martin Thwait

records.account_note: Sells unwanted bulk items (lenses, unstrung bows, skilling products) at high-alch price without runes. Alternatives for many items at once: Void Knight General Store, Jennifer's General Supplies (best given low change value). Shop channel, iron-realizable.
records.source_section: Early Money Making (prose) + Selling Items to Shops (prose)
records.value_note: High-alch-equivalent shop price; qualitative. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: High Level Alchemy (convert items to coins) - primary mid-late income

outputs.gold: high-alch value of items minus rune cost (~200 coins / 5 fire + 1 nature)

records.account_note: Most reliable mid-late conversion of items to coins. Profitable if high-alch value > ~200 coins (rune shop cost). Notable alchables: adamant/rune/dragon equipment, elemental battlestaves, dragonhide equipment, rune & dragon javelin tips, onyx bolt tips (-> onyx bolts (e)), rune arrows, jewellery, high-level Fletching (yew longbows). Most efficient alched during other activities (Agility, travel, Sailing). high_alch channel, iron-realizable.
records.value_note: Per-item; profit threshold ~200 coins rune cost. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Skilling for alchables (Crafting / Smithing / Hallowed Sepulchre & Wilderness Agility Course / Runecraft / Fletching / Sailing)

outputs.gold: via high-alch or shop sale of products

records.account_note: Umbrella prose: Crafting (gems->jewellery, battlestaves, dragonhide armour); ores/bars alched or smithed then alched; Hallowed Sepulchre & Wilderness Agility Course reward large alchable+gold quantities; Runecraft at runic altars/Ourania/Guardians of the Rift -> runes sold to shops; Fletching (bows/shields) using Miscellania/Vale Totems flax & logs; Sailing via Shipwreck Salvaging (good profit after 53). Mixed channel; iron-realizable. Specific rows captured separately (Hallowed Sepulchre, ZMI, Battlestaves).
records.value_note: Umbrella; specific hourly figures in tables. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: PVM for alchables (Slayer tasks, bosses & raids)

outputs.gold: high-alch value of dropped alchables

records.account_note: High-level Slayer tasks and bosses/raids have lucrative drop tables; many irons earn coins while farming rare drops. Realized via high-alch (NOT GE for irons). Outputs include 'drop table' aggregate pseudo-items (flagged aggregate). Iron-realizable. Specific bosses (Vorkath, Green dragons, Corrupted Gauntlet, Zombie pirates, Rune dragons) captured separately.
records.value_note: Per drop table; aggregate. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Selling extra runes to shops (TzHaar / Ali Morrisane)

outputs.gold: coins from Ali's Discount Wares (fixed prices, no overstock decrease)

records.account_note: Extra runes (Zeah RC, Barrows) sold to TzHaar shop for Tokkul or to Ali's Discount Wares / Ali Morrisane for coins at fixed prices. Shop channel, iron-realizable.
records.source_section: Mid-Late Game: Selling Items to Shops (prose)
records.value_note: Fixed shop prices. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Treasure Trails / Clue scrolls (clue casket rewards)

quests: X Marks the Spot (for scroll boxes)

outputs.resources: alchables, useful runes, unique items (Ranger boots, Blessed d'hide); clue 'reward casket' is an aggregate pseudo-item (flagged aggregate)

records.account_note: Iron-realizable value = High-alchemy column only (high_alch channel). GE Value column is GE-dependent and NOT realizable for irons (informational; mains could use GE). Rare/cosmetic non-alch items can fill Death's Coffer. Clues passively obtained via Slayer/Thieving/Fishing/Woodcutting; Combat Achievement tiers increase clue drop rates. Excludes value of master clues from lower caskets. Outputs are aggregate drop tables (flagged).
records.source_section: Alternative Money Making: Treasure Trails (prose + casket table)
records.value_note: HA values iron-realizable; GE values main-only and price-volatile. Snapshot from rendered page. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Wintertodt (Recommended table)

outputs.resources: Various logs, gems, ores, herbs, seeds, fish

records.account_note: Absolute hourly profit low; not efficient to continue past target Firemaking level. Mixed (gold + XP + resources); iron-realizable.
records.value_note: 30,000 gp/hr. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

outputs.gold: direct coins from pickpocketing

records.value_note: 127,000 gp/hr. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Battlestaves (shop-bought, crafted into elemental staves then alched)

outputs.gold: high-alch value of elemental battlestaves

records.account_note: Buy 5 battlestaves/world from Magic Guild Store (shop, iron-realizable input), add air orbs (via Superglass Make/glassblowing during normal Crafting), then high-alch. Rate assumes air orbs, 5 staves/world, glass time discounted. high_alch channel, iron-realizable.
records.value_note: 928,000 gp/hr, assumption-heavy. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Battlestaves (monster drops, crafted into elemental staves then alched)

outputs.gold: high-alch value of elemental battlestaves

records.account_note: Same processing as shop-bought row but battlestaff input from monster drops (free input). high_alch channel, iron-realizable.
records.value_note: 2,057,000 gp/hr. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Green dragons (lance setup)

outputs.gold: alchables

records.account_note: Worthwhile only before Piety unlock (big DPS) or after all upgrades (max efficiency). Realized via high-alch of dragonhide/bones-derived items + Prayer XP. high_alch channel, iron-realizable. Dragon bones aggregate resource.
records.value_note: 496,000 gp/hr. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.account_note: Lower-gear variant of green dragons. high_alch channel, iron-realizable.
records.value_note: 265,000 gp/hr. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.value_note: 94,000 gp/hr. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

outputs.resources: Uncut gems, runes, crystal shards, dragon arrows

records.account_note: Efficient chiefly because bow of Faerdhinen is a strong item most collect anyway; stop after a bow + six armour seeds. Realized via high-alch / direct resources; iron-realizable.

records.account_note: AFK Daeyalt variant. Shop channel, iron-realizable.

outputs.gold: from cut gems / diamonds processed to amulets (u) then alched

records.account_note: Profit increased by processing diamonds into amulets (u) to alch (only efficient before 87 Crafting). Realized via high-alch. Iron-realizable.

records.method: Vorkath

outputs.gold: alchables
outputs.xp: 26,000 Slayer; banked: 110,000 Cooking, 72,000 Prayer, 23,000 Crafting, 15,000 Fletching, 5,000 Smithing
outputs.resources: Dragon bolts/darts/arrows, ~150 Blue dragon scales, ~18 Manta rays, ~54 Shark lures

records.account_note: Most efficient on Slayer task. Realized via high-alch of alchables + banked processing XP/resources. high_alch channel, iron-realizable.
records.value_note: 1,500,000 gp/hr. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Zombie pirates (Recommended table, with Wilderness Medium Diary)

outputs.gold: coins + alchables
outputs.resources: ~100 Blood/death/chaos/mind runes; ~210 Blighted food, ~14 Blighted super restores, ~140 Blighted ancient ice sacks; ~35 Adamant seeds, ~400 steel cannonballs, ~70 Gold ore

records.risk: Wilderness (Blighted/PvP zone); bring cheaply-reclaimable/untradeable gear to minimise risk
records.account_note: Without Medium Wilderness Diary drops decreased 70%. Assume ~200 kills/hr without cannon. Realized via mixed (coins + alchables + resources). Iron-realizable. Wilderness risk flagged.
records.value_note: 650,000 gp/hr at ~200 kills/hr. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Processing adamantite ore (ore -> bar -> adamant sword -> high alch)

outputs.gold: high-alch value of adamant swords

records.account_note: Hourly assumes 1,200 adamantite ore already banked (~1,200 high-alch casts/hr); ~225k/hr if mining 120 ore/hr. Process: adamantite ore -> adamantite bar -> adamant sword -> high alch. high_alch channel, iron-realizable.
records.value_note: 3,150,000 gp/hr assuming pre-banked ore. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Processing runite ore (ore -> bar -> rune sword -> high alch)

outputs.gold: high-alch value of rune swords

records.account_note: Hourly assumes 1,200 runite ore already banked; ~875k/hr if mining 70 ore/hr. Caveat: converting banked runite to coins may endanger Rune Dart upkeep for Toxic blowpipe. Process: runite ore -> runite bar -> rune sword -> high alch. high_alch channel, iron-realizable.
records.value_note: 15,774,000 gp/hr assuming pre-banked ore. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Cutting gems at a gem shop

outputs.gold: profit reselling cut gems to gem shop

records.account_note: Buy uncut gems, cut, resell cut to gem shop for profit. No Wilderness; low req; medium intensity; hop gem shops for stock. Not sustainable long periods (slow restock). UIM badge: Yes. Shop channel, iron-realizable (buy+resell to NPC shop, not GE).
records.value_note: 90,000 gp/hr. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Fletching willow shortbows

outputs.gold: high-alch or general store sale of bows

records.account_note: Fletch highest available bow; high-alch or sell to general stores. Low intensity, useful, quest-relevant skills. UIM badge: Yes. Mixed (high_alch or shop). Iron-realizable.
records.value_note: 13,000 gp/hr. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Stealing from H.A.M. Store rooms

quests: Death to the Dorgeshuun (at least half completed)

outputs.gold: high-alch or bank loot (jewellery from chests)
outputs.resources: emerald rings, sapphire necklaces (save some for personal use)

records.account_note: Pickpocket guards for keys to chests with jewellery. Best low-level method for early jewellery + starting cash. Rate assumes 200 chests/hr. Full H.A.M. robes reduce kick chance in Hideout (never kicked in storeroom; rogue equipment recommended to ~double key rate). UIM badge: 'Yes with alch'. Mixed (high_alch / kept resources). Iron-realizable.
records.value_note: 67,000 gp/hr at 200 chests/hr. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Stealing silk (Ardougne market)

outputs.gold: 60 coins each sold to silk merchant after 20 min wait

records.account_note: Steal silk from Ardougne market stalls; after 20 min sell to silk merchant for 60 coins each. Assumes 400 stolen/sold per hour. UIM badge: No. Shop channel, iron-realizable.
records.value_note: 24,000 gp/hr; 60 coins/silk; 400/hr. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.value_note: 100,000+ gp/hr (200,000+ with rogue equipment). gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Selling runes from Guardians of the Rift to Ali Morrisane

outputs.gold: coins from Ali Morrisane's rune shop
outputs.resources: Nature, chaos, law, blood runes (also useful for magic/teleports)

records.account_note: GotR yields valuable catalytic runes sold to Ali Morrisane for coins. RC level reqs gainable via Enter the Abyss / Temple of the Eye. Increases as higher-tier catalytic runes unlock. UIM badge: Yes. Shop channel, iron-realizable.
records.value_note: 60,000 gp/hr, ~30,000 RC XP/hr. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Giants' Foundry

items: bars / equipment bought from shops (bronze+iron or iron+steel)
requirements.note: Smithing 15 up to iron, 30 up to steel

outputs.gold: coins (depending on alloys; can profit or loss)

records.account_note: Minigame; profit/loss depends on alloys. Cheapest bar input = claws (mithril claws from Jatizso's Weapons galore). To profit from purchased equipment: buy bronze+iron (Smithing 15) or iron+steel (Smithing 30); better moulds increase profit. UIM badge: Yes (run/teleport to Al Kharid for platelegs/plateskirts). Shop channel, iron-realizable (buy from NPC shops, smith via minigame).
records.value_note: 92,000 gp/hr. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: High alching rune arrows from Last Man Standing

requirements.note: Runecraft 44 recommended to craft own nature runes

outputs.gold: high-alch value of rune arrows (240 coins each)

records.account_note: LMS PvP minigame, no death risk. Justine's shop sells 300 rune arrows for 3 points; high-alch for 240 each. Strategy yields 4-3 points/game. Assumes 6 LMS points + 600 self-crafted nature runes/hr -> 144,000 coins/hr (vs 72,000 if RC'ing natures, 12,000 if buying natures). Skiller alt: 350 adamant arrows/point sold to Brian (Rimmington) 50/world. UIM badge: Yes. high_alch channel, iron-realizable.
records.value_note: 144,000 gp/hr (assumes self-RC nature runes). gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.account_note: Mine dense essence, venerate, run to Blood Altar; 212 blood runes/run sold to Ali Morrisane @200 ea. Up to 460,000 coins/hr + 38,000 RC XP with optimised runs. UIM badge: Yes. Shop channel, iron-realizable.
records.value_note: 330,000 gp/hr (up to 460,000 optimised); 200 coins/blood rune fixed. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Blast mining and smithing runite (ore -> bar -> rune items -> high alch)

outputs.gold: high-alch value of rune items

records.account_note: Blast mining gives runite at lvl 75 (bulk of profit). Needs lots of dynamite (CoX, or 80/day from Thirus with Kourend&Kebos elite diary; buying not recommended). Smelt at Blast Furnace, smith runite bars to rune items (e.g. rune swords), high-alch. UIM badge: Maybe. high_alch channel, iron-realizable.
records.value_note: 650,000 gp/hr. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.method: Crafting amethyst javelin tips (mine amethyst -> craft tips -> high alch)

outputs.gold: 364,800-456,000 (700k-900k with tick manipulation) coins of javelin tips high-alched

records.account_note: Mine amethyst, craft amethyst javelin tips, high-alch. 80-100 amethyst/hr standard, 160-200 with 3t tick manip -> 400-500 tips/hr. Low inventory need (good for UIM). Varrock Elite Diary (Varrock armour 4) gives extra amethyst chance; Expert mining gloves (25% no-deplete); Celestial ring +4 Mining. UIM badge: Yes. high_alch channel, iron-realizable.
records.value_note: 364,000 (364,800-456,000; 700k-900k tick-manip) gp/hr. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.

records.account_note: Relatively low effort, lucrative; runite bars + valuable alchables. UIM badge: Yes. high_alch channel, iron-realizable.
records.value_note: 1,000,000 gp/hr in alchables, net of supplies. gp values are price-volatile snapshots from the OSRS Wiki guide; high-alch/shop prices are fixed but coin worth assumptions may drift.
